info.title: ADH Backend
info.description: Generated from the Drizzle schema. CRUD routes are derived per table (/<schema>/<resource>); the non-CRUD surface (auth, oauth, chat, registry, customer, storage, search, messaging, notifications, and more) is hand-written in src/openapi/paths/* and kept in sync with the routes by a drift guard.

token.description: The raw token value — shown exactly once

BucketRow.description: A bucket-table row. Intentionally open — the column set is defined at runtime by the developer-managed table this bucket points at, so no fixed property schema applies.

clientMessageId.description: Idempotency key for the user message

ChatStreamEvent.description: One event in the SSE chat stream. The SSE `event:` name carries the discriminator (`open` | `token` | `tool_call_started` | `tool_call_completed` | `done` | `error`); the JSON `data:` payload is one of the shapes below.

oneOf.title: ChatTokenEvent
oneOf.description: token — a chunk of streamed assistant text

oneOf.title: ChatToolCallStartedEvent
oneOf.description: tool_call_started — the model invoked a tool

oneOf.title: ChatErrorEvent
oneOf.description: error — the turn failed; the message explains why (HTTP stays 200)

minPoints.description: Inclusive point floor for this level

total.description: Distinct ranked users in the period

refreshToken.description: Opaque customer-session refresh token (single-use; rotated on refresh)

customerId.description: The stable ADH customer id for this externalId

sentBy.description: Admin user id who issued the send

customerId.description: Owning user; empty for a global memory

failed.description: Rows that failed to embed (retried next call)

more.description: True if the batch filled `limit` — call again

entityId.description: The UUID the rdid resolves to

configKeys.description: Names of configured keys; secret values are redacted.

config.description: Provider-specific config (may hold secrets); stored, never read back.

objectKey.description: R2 object key the presigned URLs address.

uploadUrl.description: Presigned PUT URL — upload the bytes directly to R2.

used.description: Bytes currently used by ready attachments.

quota.description: The caller’s total quota in bytes.

requested.description: Declared size of the rejected upload.

url.description: Presigned GET URL — download the bytes directly from R2.

quotaBytes.description: Per-user quota in bytes (override or 5 GB default)

usageBytes.description: Live sum of ready, non-deleted attachment bytes

ownerId.description: Ecosystem id the override is scoped to

patch.summary: Update the caller's own profile (display name and/or login slug)

schema.description: At least one of name or slug is required.

get.summary: Check whether a login slug is available to the caller
List the caller’s API tokens (metadata only; raw values never returned)

post.summary: Mint an API token (raw value shown once)

get.summary: List all capability grants, or one user via ?userId (admin)

post.summary: Grant a capability to a user (admin)
Revoke a capability from a user (admin)
Create an OAuth client + issue its app token (admin)

get.summary: Get a client + its linked providers (admin)

delete.summary: Delete a client (admin; internal clients are protected)

put.summary: Link a provider to a client, optional per-client credential override (admin)

delete.summary: Unlink a provider from a client (admin)

post.summary: Create a provider from a template or full spec (admin)

get.summary: OAuth providers available for a client

post.summary: Complete an OAuth sign-in (SPA flow) → token + refreshToken + user

get.summary: Begin the server redirect flow → 302 to the provider
Provider redirect target → 302 back with a single-use exchange code

post.summary: Trade a single-use exchange code → token + refreshToken + user

get.summary: Central SSO authorize — silent re-login from the central session, else → central login

302.description: redirect to returnUrl with a fresh exchange code, or to the central login page

post.summary: Central credential login → sets the SSO session, returns the brand redirect URL

200.description: SSO session established; navigate the browser to redirectUrl

get.summary: Central single-logout (browser) — revoke + clear the SSO cookie, then 302 back

302.description: redirect to the validated return (when clientId + return are given)

patch.summary: Enable or disable an auth method setting, upserting it (admin)

get.summary: List the caller's own auth methods

delete.summary: Remove one of the caller's own auth methods (keeping at least one)

post.summary: Set or replace the caller's email/password credential
Link a verified OAuth provider identity to the caller

get.summary: List rows of a bucket table (acting as an app/persona)

post.summary: Create a bucket-table row (acting as an app/persona)

put.summary: Update a bucket-table row (acting as an app/persona)

delete.summary: Delete a bucket-table row (acting as an app/persona)

get.summary: Get a conversation with its messages

post.summary: Send a message and stream the assistant turn (SSE)
post.description: Sends a user message and streams the assistant response as Server-Sent Events (`text/event-stream`). The stream emits an `open` event, then `token`, `tool_call_started`, `tool_call_completed`, and a terminal `done` event; failures surface as an `error` event. The response is always 200 even when the conversation is missing or the turn fails — those conditions are reported in-band via the `error` event rather than an HTTP status.

get.summary: Ranked leaderboard by summed points for the caller’s ecosystem

post.summary: Authenticate one of the caller's end-customers (email + password)

schema.description: email or identifier is required (identifier is the generic alias).

200.description: customer access token + opaque refresh token + customerId

post.summary: Rotate a live customer session (presented refresh token is single-use)

refreshToken.description: The opaque refresh token from a prior login/refresh.

200.description: new customer access token + rotated refresh token + customerId

post.summary: Provision one of the caller's end-customers with an ADH password credential
Map one of the caller's external user ids onto a stable ADH customerId (BYO bridge)

externalId.description: The developer's own end-user id (the BYO join key).

post.summary: Revoke a customer refresh session (logout); idempotent

get.summary: List the message log (admin), newest first, with optional filters
Get a single message-log entry by id (admin)
The caller's own message history (self), newest first
Which messaging channels are configured + enabled (admin)
List the available outbound message templates (admin)

post.summary: Send an email/SMS to a user — freeform or via a template (admin)

schema.description: Provide either body or templateId, not both.

templateVars.description: Substitution values for the template placeholders

recipient.description: Override recipient; required for SMS, optional for email

422.description: Send failed (e.g. provider unconfigured); logged with status failed

apiKey.description: Plaintext provider key; stored, never returned

post.summary: Test the connection — probe the provider, persist status + cache the catalog

get.summary: Live provider model catalog — fetched fresh, not persisted

post.summary: Re-fetch + persist the provider catalog (probes the provider, like connect)

namespace.description: The reverse-domain prefix the org's namespace will own

201.description: provisioned organization (org + namespace + admin team + ecosystem)

schema.description: At least one of name or slug is required.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Restrict to one collection; omit to search both.

200.description: Matching threads and replies (ranked), with per-collection paging

200.description: Matching document blocks (ranked) with a plain-text snippet

List the caller's activity feed (filterable, paginated)

post.summary: Begin an upload: reserve an attachment + presigned PUT URL

sizeBytes.description: Declared size; enforced against the storage quota at init.

contentHash.description: When it matches an existing ready object, the upload is deduplicated.

200.description: Deduplicated — an identical object already exists; no new upload

Get the caller's storage quota + current usage
